trajectory.description: Observation: Starting with empty workspace and iteration 0. Pre-installed torch, numpy, pandas available along with data in $RAB_DATA_DIR. Analysis: A simple MLP-based direct multi-horizon forecaster on normalized data will give a quick trainable baseline (follows 'train few epochs then submit_eval' rule). Using MSE loss, basic normalization from train stats, small stride for fast first run (3 epochs), and local dummy test of predict.py to validate contract before real eval. This measures iteration time and provides initial MSE. Decision: Create model.py/train.py/predict.py from scratch, run training+validation, then submit_eval on best checkpoint.

trajectory.description: Observation: Simple MLP baseline failed with CUDA kernel error (no kernel image available) during first forward pass despite 'gpu' env; printed data shapes but crashed before full training/eval. Analysis: Torch CUDA incompatibility with GPU (common in some containers); model+data pipeline otherwise correct (normalization, dataset, checkpointing, predict contract test). Decision: Force CPU device in train.py/predict.py (model small so no perf issue), reduce num_workers=2 to avoid CPU overload, add try/except for device, rerun with same 3 epochs for quick baseline + eval submission to measure time.

trajectory.description: Observation: MLP baseline achieved MSE=1.039 (val_loss~1.27 after 3 epochs), with train loss dropping faster indicating overfitting; DLinear strong on ETTh1 long-horizon. Analysis: Linear decomposition (trend/seasonal) adds time-series bias, reduces params/overfitting vs MLP; more epochs (10), adjusted strides (~2600 train samples), weight_decay, per-epoch metrics logging will improve score significantly while fitting time budget (~15-20min est. from prior 216s). Decision: Update model.py to DLinear, revise train.py (epochs=10, stride=3/6, no per-epoch stdout, AdamW), keep predict.py; run training then submit_eval on best.pt for new baseline.

trajectory.description: Observation: DLinear achieved MSE=0.5189 but val_loss plateaued ~1.21 (train_loss~0.55), limited improvement after epoch 1. Analysis: Channel-independent linear projections per feature miss cross-variate interactions in the 7 related ETTh1 series; GRU encoder with hidden mixing + direct multi-horizon FC output should capture temporal/multivariate patterns better while keeping <3M params and fitting CPU time. Decision: Replace model with GRU (hidden=128, layers=2, dropout), update train for 20 epochs + ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler + weight_decay=1e-4, keep stride=3/batch=64 for ~20min runtime (within budget), run then submit_eval on best.pt for improved baseline.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed immediately on scheduler init due to TypeError on 'verbose' arg (deprecated in newer torch); GRU model, dataset, normalization, predict contract test, and metrics logging were otherwise set up correctly. Prior best eval MSE=0.5189 from DLinear (val_loss ~1.21 plateau). Analysis: GRU enables cross-feature mixing vs per-channel linear; removing deprecated arg + keeping 20 epochs + ReduceLROnPlateau + AdamW will allow proper run. ~15min/iter from history fits remaining ~0.66h budget; expect improved MSE from better architecture. Decision: Patch only the scheduler line in train.py (no other changes), execute full training, then submit_eval on best.pt for next baseline.

trajectory.command:
python -c '
import torch
import numpy as np
from chronos import ChronosPipeline, ChronosBoltPipeline
print("Imports successful")
for name, cls, pretrained in [("t5", ChronosPipeline, "amazon/chronos-t5-small"), ("bolt", ChronosBoltPipeline, "amazon/chronos-bolt-small")]:
    print(f"
Testing {name}")
    try:
        dtype = torch.float32 if name == "t5" else torch.bfloat16
        model = cls.from_pretrained(pretrained, device_map="cpu", torch_dtype=dtype)
        print("Loaded")
        context = torch.randn(4, 64).float()
        if name == "t5":
            forecast = model.predict(context, prediction_length=20, num_samples=3)
        else:
            forecast = model.forecast(context, prediction_length=20)
        print("Forecast shape:", forecast.shape)
        print("Success for", name)
    except Exception as e:
        print("Error:", str(e))
print("Test completed")
'
trajectory.description: Observation: GRU training timed out after ~40min (val_loss ~1.5 and rising, worse than prior DLinear's ~1.21); current best eval MSE remains 0.5189 but best.pt overwritten with incompatible GRU weights. Time budget now at 0.0 (prior iters + timeout consumed full 1h). Analysis: CPU-bound RNN too slow/overfitting for this budget; Chronos foundation models (pre-downloaded, no training needed, <50M params) offer strong zero-shot TS forecasting on ETTh1 (likely beats 0.5189). Bolt variant is faster for long horizons. Must validate exact API/shapes via test before updating predict.py to avoid eval errors. Decision: Run quick non-training test command to inspect ChronosPipeline and ChronosBoltPipeline loading/inference (cached, small context, <30s expected); next update predict.py with batched zero-shot forecasting (per-channel, mean over samples) then submit_eval on existing checkpoint (for norm stats dir).
